paper_title: Study of Diatom Assemblages in Southern Poland Rivers

methods.method_name: Field Sampling and Water Parameter Measurement
methods.description: This method involves collecting water samples from various river sites to measure physical and chemical parameters, which are crucial for understanding the environmental conditions affecting diatom assemblages.

steps.description: Samples were collected four times per sampling site in 2017 (spring, early and late summer, autumn and winter) to determine the physical and chemical parameters of the water.
steps.searchable_summary: collect water samples from river sites seasonally

steps.description: Conductivity, total dissolved solids, pH, and temperature of the water were measured in the field using a HI 9811-5 pH/EC/TDS/°C meter (Hanna Instruments) and dissolved oxygen was measured using a CO-401 oxygen meter (Elmetron).
steps.searchable_summary: measure water parameters using field meters

steps.description: Salinity was measured using a WTW meter and the result was expressed as the total dissolved solids converted from the measurements of electrical conductivity.

For high anthropogenic impact sites (conductivity > 1000 μS.cm-1), a conversion factor of 0.72 × conductivity was applied, while for low impact sites (conductivity < 1000 μS.cm-1), the conversion factor was 0.77 × conductivity.
steps.searchable_summary: apply conversion factor to conductivity based on impact level

steps.description: Analyses of chlorides, sulfates, iron, nutrients, calcium, magnesium, total hardness, and alkalinity were performed in the laboratory according to standard methods.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze chemical components in water samples

steps.description: The morphometry of the riverbed, i.e., the average width of the channel, the depth of the river, and the flow velocity were also performed in the field.
steps.searchable_summary: measure riverbed morphometry in the field

methods.description: This method describes the collection and preparation of diatom samples for taxonomic analysis to identify species and assess their abundance and ecological metrics.

steps.description: Diatoms were taken from a 25 cm² surface of the bottom sediments or concrete slabs at each study site.
steps.searchable_summary: collect diatom samples from riverbed surfaces

steps.description: Permanent slides for light microscopy were prepared following the standard protocol.
Samples were treated with 10% hydrochloric acid to remove calcium carbonate and boiled in 37% hydrogen peroxide to eliminate organic matter.
steps.searchable_summary: treat samples with acid and peroxide

steps.description: After washing four times with distilled water, the final suspension was pipetted onto cover slips, left to evaporate, and mounted on glass slides using Naphrax® diatom mountant.
Slides were examined using light microscopes, and measurements and photographic documentation were performed using AxioVision software.
A minimum of 400 valves were identified to the species or variety level, and their relative abundance was determined.
Ecological metrics such as species richness, Shannon index, and Evenness index were calculated.

methods.description: This method involves statistical analysis to evaluate differences in environmental variables and multivariate analysis to relate diatom species composition to environmental factors.

steps.description: The significance of differences in median values of environmental variables among sampling sites was calculated using the Kruskal-Wallis one-way ANOVA and post hoc tests.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Kruskal-Wallis ANOVA on environmental data

steps.description: Canonical ordination analyses for relating diatom species composition to environmental variables were carried out using CANOCO software.
A unimodal direct ordination canonical correspondence analysis (CCA) with forward selection was used to reduce the large set of environmental variables.
The relationship between diatom species and environmental variables was evaluated using the Monte Carlo permutation test.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate species-environment relationship with Monte Carlo test

steps.description: The structures of diatom flora and relationships between assemblages were analyzed using PRIMER software with cluster analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze diatom assemblages with cluster analysis

steps.description: Ranked similarity matrices were constructed using the Bray-Curtis similarity measure with a fourth root transform and group-average sorting.
steps.searchable_summary: construct similarity matrices with Bray-Curtis measure
